content:
Sure, I can help you with that!

Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges you provided:

* [(0, 2, 0), (0, 3, 1), (1, 2, 5), (3, 1, 6)]

We need to find the pairs of nodes that are linked together at each time step.

At time 0, node 0 and node 3 are linked.

At time 1, node 1 and node 2 are linked.

At time 5, node 5 and node 1 are linked.

At time 6, node 3 and node 6 are linked.

At time 7, node 7 and node 13 are linked.

At time 10, node 10 and node 12 are linked.

At time 11, node 11 and node 17 are linked.

At time 12, node 12 and node 24 are linked.

At time 13, node 13 and node 15 are linked.

At time 14, node 14 and node 19 are linked.

At time 15, node 15 and node 20 are linked.

At time 16, node 16 and node 22 are linked.

At time 17, node 17 and node 23 are linked.

At time 18, node 18 and node 21 are linked.

At time 19, node 19 and node 24 are linked.

At time 20, node 20 and node 4 are linked.

At time 21, node 21 and node 1 are linked.

At time 22, node 22 and node 5 are linked.

At time 23, node 23 and node 7 are linked.

At time 24, node 24 and node 10 are linked.

Now, when are node 14 and node 15 linked?

The answer is: At time 13, node 14 and node 15 are linked.
